claimText: Under Co-Lending Arrangements between banks and NBFCs, each Regulated Entity must retain a minimum 10 percent share of the loans.
evidenceExcerpt: Co-lending norms require each Regulated Entity to retain a minimum 10 percent share of the loans.
examUse: Statement-based question on Co-lending norms and risk retention.
distractorHooks: Only the lead bank must retain a share

claimText: Lenders in a Co-Lending Arrangement can claim Priority Sector Lending status for their respective share of the loans.
evidenceExcerpt: Lenders can claim Priority Sector Lending status for their share.
examUse: Statement-based question on Priority Sector Lending eligibility in co-lending.
distractorHooks: Co-lending loans are ineligible for PSL status
Only banks can claim PSL status for the entire loan amount

claimText: Co-lenders must uniformly classify assets; if one lender tags a loan as a Non-Performing Asset (NPA), the other lenders must do the same.
evidenceExcerpt: Co-lenders must uniformly classify assets; an NPA tag by one requires the same by others.
examUse: Statement-based question on NPA classification rules in joint lending.
distractorHooks: Independent asset classification applies to each lender
NPA classification is decided only by the majority stakeholder

claimText: Non-bank Payment Aggregators must obtain RBI authorisation under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
evidenceExcerpt: Non-bank Payment Aggregators must obtain RBI authorisation under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
examUse: Statement-based question on regulatory frameworks for Payment Aggregators.
distractorHooks: Authorised under the Banking Regulation Act
Regulated by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) instead of RBI
